item.title: 7.1 magnitude earthquake strikes Venezuela, triggers tsunami advisory
item.description: A preliminary 7.1 magnitude earthquake was reported Wednesday evening in northwest Venezuela, according to the U.S. Geological Survey.

item.title: How Democratic Socialist primary wins in New York could shape upcoming elections
item.description: New York City Democratic Socialist Mayor Zohran Mamdani flexed his political muscle on Tuesday, with all three of his endorsed political candidates winning their House primaries. CBS News' Fin Gómez has more.

item.title: Northern California hit by 5.6-magnitude earthquake in Mendocino County
item.description: A 5.6-magnitude earthquake struck Mendocino County on Wednesday morning, triggering ShakeAlert notifications across Northern California.

item.title: Examining Trump's decision to cancel bipartisan housing bill signing
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday canceled plans to sign a bipartisan housing bill, demanding that lawmakers first pass the SAVE Act. Political strategists Ameshia Cross and Tricia McLaughlin join with analysis.

item.title: Trump and Cassidy have spat in testy meeting, sources say
item.description: President Trump had a testy meeting with Senate Republicans on Wednesday. Sen. Bill Cassidy of Louisiana said he lost his temper and Trump raised his voice, but the president said, "We have a really well unified party." CBS News congressional reporter Taurean Small has more.

item.title: Some states want to regulate prediction markets. Should the feds let them?
item.description: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission has sued nine states to block them from regulating prediction markets such as Kalshi and Polymarket.

item.title: Polymarket launches probe after Wall Street Journal investigation alleges deceptive marketing
item.description: A new report from The Wall Street Journal alleges that Polymarket paid content creators to post videos about winning bets that did not exist. Caitlin Ostroff, one of the reporters who broke the story, joins "The Takeout" to discuss.

item.title: Trump admin. asks Congress for $88B in supplemental funding for Iran war, other needs
item.description: The Trump administration on Wednesday sent Congress a long-awaited supplemental funding package to help cover the cost of the Iran war.

item.title: Cait Conley on her race against Rep. Mike Lawler in November
item.description: In November, Republican Rep. Mike Lawler will face combat veteran and Biden administration national security staffer Cait Conley, who prevailed in Tuesday night's Democratic primary for New York's 17th congressional district. Conley joins "The Takeout" to discuss why she thinks she can flip the seat from red to blue.

item.title: Air Canada flight diverted to Logan Airport due to "incapacitated" captain
item.description: An Air Canada flight out of Newark, New Jersey, was forced to divert and land at Boston after the captain became "incapacitated" and a co-pilot had to take over.

item.title: Are Democrats having a Tea Party moment?
item.description: Progressive candidates endorsed by New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani emerged victorious in Tuesday's primaries. CBS News' Fin Gómez and Anthony Salvanto join with analysis.

item.title: Meta creating prediction market app, NYT says
item.description: Prediction markets like Kalshi and Polymarket, which allow people to bet on nearly anything, are big business, and Meta is hoping to get in on the action, according to a new report from the New York Times. CNET senior technology reporter Abrar Al-Heeti joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: Trump and Cassidy had testy exchange during meeting with GOP senators, sources say
item.description: President Trump had a testy meeting with Republican senators focused mostly on the Iran war, sources say. CBS News' Taurean Small and Nikole Killion have the latest.

item.title: New York Democratic primaries show shift away from establishment
item.description: New York voters in three closely-watched primaries rejected candidates backed by Democratic leaders in Congress on Tuesday night. CBS News chief Washington correspondent Major Garrett has more.

item.title: CBS News poll finds more oppose than favor data centers in their area
item.description: Most Americans expect data centers to negatively impact the environment, local resources, although some see economic benefits

item.title: Takeaways from wins for Mamdani-backed candidates
item.description: All three candidates endorsed by New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani won their primary races on Tuesday. CBS News political reporter Zak Hudak has more on what this means nationally.

item.title: Bill Gates said Epstein tried to use knowledge of his affairs "to pressure me"
item.description: Bill Gates testified June 10 for nearly six hours before the House Oversight Committee, which is examining the government's handling of the Epstein case and those with ties to him.

item.title: U.N. nuclear agency chief says Iran inspections will happen, at some point
item.description: After conflicting remarks from Iran and the U.S., the U.N. nuclear agency chief says Iranian sites will be inspected, but the timing is "not essential."

item.title: Watch: Trump meets with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte at Oval Office
item.description: President Trump hosted NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte at the White House on Wednesday. The pair discussed defense spending and more.

item.title: Trump calls for probe into gas price gouging
item.description: President Trump is calling on the Department of Justice to investigate what he calls gas price gouging at the pump. Patrick De Haan, head of petroleum analysis at GasBuddy, joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: Camp Mystic files for bankruptcy almost a year after deadly Texas floods
item.description: Camp Mystic listed its debt as exceeding $10 million.

item.title: Trump cancels housing bill signing, reiterates demand for SAVE America Act
item.description: President Trump canceled a planned signing ceremony on Wednesday for a housing affordability bill that passed Congress by wide bipartisan margins.

item.title: Did a father use his 6-year-old son as an alibi for murder?
item.description: Reginald Reed Sr. said he was playing video games with his son, Reginald "Reggie" Reed Jr., when his wife, Selonia Reed, was killed. But detectives doubt his story.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Espaillat's loss a political earthquake for House Dems, Hispanic caucus
item.description: Five-term incumbent Rep. Adriano Espaillat was defeated in the Democratic primary Tuesday night in a stunning upset by political newcomer and democratic socialist Darializa Avila Chevalier.

item.title: Federal court allows ICE to expand expedited deportations nationwide
item.description: A federal appeals court has allowed the Trump administration to move forward with an effort to expand fast-track deportations throughout the U.S.

item.title: FBI, NYPD carry out searches tied to bribery investigation of police officials
item.description: The searches stemmed from an ongoing probe into the conduct of former NYPD Chief of Department Jeffrey Maddrey, a source told CBS News.

item.title: Trump has testy meeting with GOP senators, telling Cassidy to sit down
item.description: President Trump met with Republican senators soon after canceling plans to sign bipartisan housing affordability legislation at the Capitol.

item.title: Wounded soldiers, families accuse Army of downplaying war injuries
item.description: Soldiers say the Army disregarded warnings about thin defenses and ignored requests for medical supplies. Now they question whether the Army is being transparent about their injuries.

item.title: Mamdani influences New York City's congressional primaries with 3 big wins
item.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani endorsed three left-of-center candidates in the congressional Democratic primaries, and all three are set to win, CBS News projects.
